title: homalium axillare subsp. delphinense
intro: Homalium axillare subsp. delphinense, also known as a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, and has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Homalium axillare subsp. delphinense include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
